title: Add 'retire stale duplicates' step to future schema-set migration tooling
description: The 2026-05-12 investigation into the openclaw/discord shadow-profile hypothesis found that the 5 operator shadows in collectives/wrkstrm/private/universal/identity/ were artifacts of rismay's own 2026-03-23 e7e34f2a commit 'Migrate identities from 0.5.0 to 0.6.0'. That commit placed operator profiles inside the wrkstrm collective per the doctrine at the time, then the doctrine evolved — operators moved to substrate/operators/[slug]/ — and the migration tooling did not include a 'retire stale duplicates' step to clean up the old wrkstrm-collective copies. Result: shadow files sat unchanged from Feb 2026 onward until the 2026-05-12 cleanup. Doctrine recommendation: future schema-set migration tooling (whichever Koma owns this when it comes online) should include a step that checks for stale duplicates when canonical home semantics change between sets and either migrates them, retires them, or surfaces them for operator decision.
